content:
I am a Postdoc at Amazon Research Berlin, where I work with Matthias Seeger and Cédric Archambeau. I am also part of the Ellis PostDoc Program. Prior to Amazon, I did my PhD at the University of Freiburg under the supervision of Frank Hutter.
My research focuses on the development of new algorithms that are able to automatically design and optimize machine learning models. Together with my collaborators from the University of Freiburg I won the ChaLearn AutoML Challenge in 2015.
News  June 2020: Together with Giovanni Zappella and Arber Zela, we organize a series of virtual seminars on AutoML June 2020: We organized a tutorial at CVPR 2020 about HPO and NAS January 2020: I successfully defended my Phd thesis November 2019: I will co-organize a workshop on neural architecture search at ICLR 2020. Recordings are available here September 2019: Our paper ‘Meta-Surrogate Benchmarking for Hyperparameter Optimization’ got accepted at NeurIPS 2019 July 2019: I started as an applied scientist at Amazon Berlin
summary: I am a Postdoc at Amazon Research Berlin, where I work with Matthias Seeger and Cédric Archambeau. I am also part of the Ellis PostDoc Program. Prior to Amazon, I did my PhD at the University of Freiburg under the supervision of Frank Hutter.
title: Aaron Klein

summary: AutoGluon is a framework for distributed hyperparameter optimization and neural architecture search. It contains several state-of-the-art methods, such as asynchronous Bayesian optimization, Hyperband as well as our recently proposed [asynchronous BOHB method](https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.10865).
title: Autogluon

summary: To accelerate the empirical evaluation of hyperparameter optimization and neural architecture search methods, we conducted an exhaustive search for feed forward neural networks ([HPO-Bench](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.04970)) and convolutional neural networks ([NASBench101](http://proceedings.mlr.press/v97/ying19a.html)) and stored all results in a database. To quickly try out a new method or feature, one can simply look up results in this database instead of training and evaluation an expensive neural network. I have some code [here](https://github.com/automl/nas_benchmarks) to use the tabular benchmarks and scripts to run popular optimizers on these benchmarks.
title: Tabular Benchmarks for HPO and NAS

summary: Emukit is a python framework for decision making under uncertainty, which includes several research directions, such as Bayesian optimization, Bayesian quadrature or experimental design. Besides providing several state-of-the-art approaches, Emukit also contains implementations for Fabolas and Profet.
title: Emukit
NAS-Bench-101: Towards Reproducible Neural Architecture Search
Tabular Benchmarks for Joint Architecture and Hyperparameter Optimization
BOHB: Robust and Efficient Hyperparameter Optimization at Scale
Fast Bayesian Optimization of Machine Learning Hyperparameters on Large Datasets
Learning Curve Prediction with Bayesian Neural Networks
Bayesian Optimization with Robust Bayesian Neural Networks
Efficient and Robust Automated Machine Learning
